title: facility — compliance evidence
description: Facility module is financial-record-adjacent. Every booking, day-pass, and pass write is a compliance-relevant financial event. SOC 2 CC6.3 (approval + segregation of duties), PCI-DSS scope (never store CVV / PAN; payment_method categories only; payment references upstream Finance module records), GDPR data-minimisation (anonymous walk-in redaction at 90 days), and WCAG 2.2 AA on the booking widget.

controls.evidence: Anonymous day-pass profile fields (name + phone) captured at issuance for operational necessity (lost-property callbacks, safety incidents). Redacted to [REDACTED] 90 days after consumption by RedactAnonymousDayPassProfileJob. Registered users retain their normal user-profile relationship.
Booking creation implies a contract of use (or a pre-existing membership contract). No consent flag required for booking-level PII (booked_for_user_id is a User FK, not raw PII).
Users see their own bookings + passes via /api/v1/bookings + /api/v1/passes with the own-only default filter. Downstream Wave 5 export flow surfaces the full history.

TenantErased cascades hard-delete of every facility + booking + day_pass + pass row via FK CASCADE. Audit trail rows survive per financial retention (Art. 17 Art. 3 exception: legal obligation to retain financial records).
The `payment_method` field on day_passes is a CATEGORY (cash / card / wallet / free / comp) — never a card number. Payment references upstream Finance module tokens (Wave 4), never inline card data.

facilities.manage-pricing + passes.revoke + bookings.cancel-any are seeded to admin/owner only. Reception cannot revoke passes or force-cancel bookings. Platform.bookings.force-cancel is ops-only + emits a notification to the tenant admin (segregation).
All /api/v1/facilities /bookings /day-passes /passes routes are TLS-only + require auth:sanctum + entitlement middleware. Platform routes additionally require auth:platform_admin.
BookingOverlapDetected event is a P1 signal + tracked via stackra.facility.overlaps.detected counter (should stay at 0). The reconciler jobs run weekly + emit findings to the alerting pipeline.
The SERIALIZABLE overlap check is the primary mitigation for the double-booking risk. The reconciler jobs are the defense-in-depth. Every double-booking incident is a P1 with a post-mortem.

day_passes.payment_method is a category enum (cash / card / wallet / free / comp) — never a card number. No CVV, no PAN, no expiry, no cardholder name stored on any facility row. Payment references upstream Finance module (Wave 4) tokens only.

Every write to facilities / resource_bookings / day_passes / passes emits an audit row (Auditable trait). Financial-record retention: 7 years. Every mutation to pricing_json + status transition + refund is captured.

The available-slots calendar widget + booking-status chip use HeroUI's design tokens which meet AA contrast by default. Status chip colours (pending / confirmed / cancelled / no-show / completed) do not rely on colour alone — each carries a text label.
Slot picker + booking-create modal are fully keyboard-navigable per HeroUI ComboBox + Calendar semantics.
Booking-create response uses aria-live=polite on the success/error message. Overlap-detection failures return an inline error at the slot picker (not a modal, per WCAG 3.3.4 error prevention).
